title: At the Hotel
description: Check in, ask about your room, and handle requests at a Filipino hotel or resort.
situation: Accommodation: checking in, room questions, and asking the front desk for help.

phrases.tip: Check in and check out are borrowed straight from English.

phrases.native: May aircon po ba ang kwarto?
phrases.english: Does the room have air conditioning?
phrases.pronunciation: may AYR-kon po ba ang KWAR-to
phrases.tip: In a tropical climate, aircon is a very common question.

phrases.native: Pwede po bang dagdagan ang tuwalya?
phrases.pronunciation: PWE-de po bang dag-DA-gan ang too-WAL-ya

phrases.tip: Sira means broken or spoiled, a useful all-purpose word.

phrases.native: Pwede po bang lumipat ng kwarto?
phrases.pronunciation: PWE-de po bang loo-MEE-pat nang KWAR-to

phrases.native: Anong oras po ang check out?
Pwede po bang mag-iwan ng bag?
phrases.english: Can I leave my bag here?
phrases.pronunciation: PWE-de po bang mag-EE-wan nang bag
phrases.tip: Handy if you check out before your flight.

phrases.english: Thank you for the great service
phrases.tip: Filipinos take great pride in warm hospitality.
